page.content: The table presents data on Leishmania spp. and strain alongside their respective IC50 (95% CI), μM values. For Old World strains, L. donovani archibaldi 2211 (MHOM/SD/91/D1783; LEM 2211) shows an IC50 of 8.0 (6.2–10.5), L. donovani donovani sudanese 1S2D (MHOM/SD/62/1S-CL2D) has an IC50 of 4.0 (2.7–5.7), L. donovani infantum LV9 (MHOM/ET/67/HU3) exhibits an IC50 of 3.0 (1.6–6.4)*, and L. major LV39 (MRHO/Sv/59/P) has an IC50 of 1.1 (0.9–1.3).

strains, L. donovani archibaldi 2211 (MHOM/SD/91/D1783; LEM 2211) shows an IC50 of 8.0 (6.2–10.5), L. donovani
donovani sudanese 1S2D (MHOM/SD/62/1S-CL2D) has an IC50 of 4.0 (2.7–5.7), L. donovani infantum LV9
(MHOM/ET/67/HU3) exhibits an IC50 of 3.0 (1.6–6.4)*, and L. major LV39 (MRHO/Sv/59/P) has an IC50 of 1.1 (0.9–1.3).

page.html:
Leishmania spp. and strainIC50 (95% CI), μM
Old World strains
L. donovani archibaldi 2211 (MHOM/SD/91/D1783; LEM 2211)8.0 (6.2–10.5)
L. donovani donovani sudanese 1S2D (MHOM/SD/62/1S-CL2D)4.0 (2.7–5.7)
L. donovani infantum LV9 (MHOM/ET/67/HU3)3.0 (1.6–6.4)*
L. major LV39 (MRHO/Sv/59/P)1.1 (0.9–1.3)
New World strains
L. mexicana amazonensis LV78 (MPRP/BR/72/M1845)5.7 (3.2–12.8)*
L. guyanensis (MHOM/SR/87/TRUUS4)3.0 (2.3–3.9)
L. mexicana (MNYC/BZ/62/M379)2.1 (1.3–3.7)
L. panamensis (MHOM/87/CO/UA140)2.7 (2.2–3.4)
L. tarantolae (TarIIWT)7.8 (6.6–9.2)

page.content: In the New World strains category, L. mexicana amazonensis LV78 (MPRP/BR/72/M1845) reports an IC50 of 5.7 (3.2–12.8)*, L. guyanensis (MHOM/SR/87/TRUUS4) has an IC50 of 3.0 (2.3–3.9), L. mexicana (MNYC/BZ/62/M379) shows an IC50 of 2.1 (1.3–3.7), L. panamensis (MHOM/87/CO/UA140) presents an IC50 of 2.7 (2.2–3.4), and L. tarantolae (TarIIWT) demonstrates an IC50 of 7.8 (6.6–9.2). This comprehensive overview encapsulates the efficacy of various Leishmania strains against their respective treatments, represented through their IC50 values with 95% confidence intervals.

L. guyanensis (MHOM/SR/87/TRUUS4) has an IC50 of 3.0 (2.3–3.9), L. mexicana (MNYC/BZ/62/M379) shows an IC50 of
2.1 (1.3–3.7), L. panamensis (MHOM/87/CO/UA140) presents an IC50 of 2.7 (2.2–3.4), and L. tarantolae (TarIIWT)
demonstrates an IC50 of 7.8 (6.6–9.2). This comprehensive overview encapsulates the efficacy of various Leishmania strains
against their respective treatments, represented through their IC50 values with 95% confidence intervals.
